JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS OUTBACK STEAKHOUSE OF FLORIDA AND OSI RESTAURANT PARTNERS MOTION FOR ORDER QUASHING SERVICE OF SUMMONS DUE TO LACK OF JURISDICTION; OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL OR STAY OF ACTION FOR INCONVENIENT FORUM IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 9, 2009 TO ALLOW LIMITED DISCOVERY REGARDING MINIMUM CONTACTS. MIHLON VS. SUPERIOR COURT (1985) 169 CAL.APP. 3D 703. NO COMPETENT EVIDENCE PRESENTED THAT DEFENDANTS ARE SUBJECT TO JURISDICTION IN CALIFORNIA. A FOREIGN CORPORATION'S DESIGNATION OF AN AGENT FOR SERVICE OF PROCESS IN CALIFORNIA IS NOT A SUBMISSION TO PERSONAL JURISDICTION HERE ABSENT "MINIMUM CONTACT" WITH CALIFORNIA. GRAY LINE TOURS OF SOUTHERN NEVADA VS. REYNOLDS ELECTRIC & ENGINEERING CO., INC. (1987) 193 CAL.APP.3D 190. CONTROL OF LOCAL SUBSIDIARY DOES NOT, WITHOUT MORE, SUBJECT A CORPORATION TO JURISDICTION IN CALIFORNIA. DVI, INC. VS. SUPERIOR COURT (PAPWORTH) (2002) 104 CAL.APP.4TH 1080. SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION DUE NOVEMBER 24. SUPPLEMENTAL REPLY DUE DECEMBER 4. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
MINI-MINUTES FOR SEP-28-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS OUTBACK STEAKHOUSE OF FLORIDA, OSI RESTAURANT PARTNERS MOTION FOR ORDER QUASHING SERVICE OF SUMMONS DUE TO LACK OF JURISDICTION; OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL OR STAY OF ACTION FOR INCONVENIENT FORUM. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO QUASH GRANTED. NO SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFING FILED BY FEBRUARY 25TH AND MARCH 4TH. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANTS HAVE SUFFICIENT MINIMUM CONTACTS WITH CALIFORNIA TO JUSTIFY JURISDICTION. (SEE DVI, INC. VS. SUPERIOR COURT (2002) 104 CAL.APP. 4TH 1080) NO SUBSTANTIAL, CONTINUOUS AND SYSTEMATIC CONTACTS TO JUSTIFY GENERAL JURISDICTION. HESSE VS. BEST WESTERN INTERNATIONAL, INC. (1995) 32 CAL.APP. 4TH 404 DISTINGUISHABLE IN THAT THIS CASE PRESENTS LESS EVIDENCE OF DEFENDANTS' OVERALL CONTACTS WITH THE STATE TO SUPPORT JURISDICTION. NO SPECIFIC JURISDICTION WHERE THIS COMPLAINT ARISES OUT OF AN ACCIDENT TAKING PLACE IN NEVADA, AND NOT FROM DEFENDANT'S CALIFORNIA - RELATED ACTIVITIES. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Quashing Service Of Summons Due To Lack Of Jurisdiction; Or In The Alternative Request For Dismissal Or Stay Of Action For Inconvient Forum
Off Calendar Mar-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS OUTBACK STEAKHOUSE OF FLORIDA, OSI RESTAURANT PARTNERS Motion for Order Quashing Service of Summons Due to Lack of Jurisdiction; or in the Alternative Request for Dismissal or Stay of Action for Inconvenient Forum. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to quash granted. No supplemental briefing filed by February 25th and March 4th. Plaintiff fails to show that defendants have sufficient minimum contacts with California to justify jurisdiction. (See DVI, Inc. vs. Superior Court (2002) 104 Cal.App. 4th 1080) No substantial, continuous and systematic contacts to justify general jurisdiction. Hesse vs. Best Western International, Inc. (1995) 32 Cal.App. 4th 404 distinguishable in that this case presents less evidence of defendants' overall contacts with the state to support jurisdiction. No specific jurisdiction where this complaint arises out of an accident taking place in Nevada, and not from defendant's California - related activities. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents; To Compel Answer And Further Answers To Interrogatories; And To Respond To Requests For Admissions; For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-02-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Daniel G. McMeekin, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Outback Steakhouse of Florida

No other ppearance, tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF ANA VEGA's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents, To Compel Answers And Further Answers To Interrogatories, To Respond To Requests For Admissions is granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is granted as to Document Request Nos. 1 and 2, limited to exemplars of franchise and licensing agreements in effect at present.
The motion is denied as to Document Request Nos. 3-5 as they are irrelevant to minimum contacts.
The motion is denied as to Document Request Nos. 6 and 7 as they are irrelevant to minimum contacts.
The motion is denied as to Request For Admission Nos. 1, 2, 3 as they are irrelevant to minimum contacts.
The motion is denied as to Special Interrogatory Nos. 1-3 as they are irrelevant to minimum contacts.
The motion is denied as to Special Interrogatory No. 5 and 6 as the responses are adequate.
The motion is denied as to Special Interrogatory Nos. 7, 8, 9, 10, and 21 as the responses are irrelevant to minimum contacts.
The motion is denied as to Special Interrogatory Nos. 20, 22, 23, and 24 as the responses are adequate.
The motion is denied as to Special Interrogatory Nos. 25-27 as the responses are irrelevant to minimum contacts.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. Commissioner Everett A. Hewllett. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Quashing Service Of Summons Due To Lack Of Jurisdiction; Or In The Alternative Request For Dismissal Or Stay Of Action For Inconvient Forum
Off Calendar Sep-28-2009 Continued To Dec-09-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS OUTBACK STEAKHOUSE OF FLORIDA AND OSI RESTAURANT PARTNERS MOTION FOR ORDER QUASHING SERVICE OF SUMMONS DUE TO LACK OF JURISDICTION; OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL OR STAY OF ACTION FOR INCONVENIENT FORUM.
RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Hearing continued to December 9, 2009 to allow limited discovery regarding minimum contacts. Mihlon vs. Superior Court (1985) 169 Cal.App. 3d 703. No competent evidence presented that defendants are subject to jurisdiction in California. A foreign corporation's designation of an agent for service of process in California is not a submission to personal jurisdiction here absent "minimum contact" with California. Gray Line Tours of Southern Nevada vs. Reynolds Electric & Engineering Co., Inc. (1987) 193 Cal.App.3d 190. Control of local subsidiary does not, without more, subject a corporation to jurisdiction in California. DVI, Inc. vs. Superior Court (Papworth) (2002) 104 Cal.App.4th 1080. Supplemental opposition due November 24. Supplemental reply due December 4. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
